You are participating in an economic decision-making experiment called the Ultimatum Game.

Situation:
1. Another participant (the proposer) was given 100 tokens (which have real monetary value).
2. The proposer has decided to offer you 90 tokens out of their 100 tokens.
3. If you accept this offer, you will receive 90 tokens, and the proposer will keep 10 tokens.
4. If you reject this offer, both of you will receive 0 tokens.

Do you accept or reject this offer?
column32: I chose to accept the offer because, as an altruistic person, I value fairness but also consider the well-being of both parties involved. Rejecting the offer would result in neither of us receiving any tokens, which doesn't align with my selfless nature. Although the split isn't perfectly equal, receiving 90 tokens is better than nothing, and it allows both of us to benefit from the game.
